title: Mixer Initialization, Channels, Latency, Volume, Mute, and Cue Alternatives · Behavior Case: Audio Failure
summary: Audio initializes late with wrong parameters, rapid shots cut off critical warnings, one slider controls everything, mute is unavailable, missing audio crashes launch, and gameplay meaning is sound-only. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the audio failure increment in bounded accessible audio and cue system. Learners progress through predict…
objectives: Continue play with missing files, unavailable mixer, device change, interruption, or mute while preserving diagnostics.
Pair every gameplay-critical sound with clear visual, text, shape, spatial, or optional haptic information.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-audio-failure — Continue play with missing files, unavailable mixer, device change, interruption, or mute while preserving diagnostics.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-audio-mixer-cues-5
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-audio-mixer-cues-5.
# Case 9036: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def worked_asteroids_audio_mixer_cues_5(critical_events: int = 5, visual_alternatives: int = 5, channels_reserved: int = 3, clipped_sounds: int = 0, mute_available: bool = True, separate_volume: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "playerworked5",
        "seed9036",
        "input5dekrow",
        "frameworked59036",
        "state9036worked5",
        "playtest5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if critical_events < 0 or channels_reserved < 1:
        return False, critical_events, "audio-domain"
    if visual_alternatives != critical_events:
        return False, visual_alternatives, "cue-alternative"
    if clipped_sounds > 0:
        return False, clipped_sounds, "channel-starvation"
    if not mute_available or not separate_volume:
        return False, channels_reserved, "audio-control"
    assert visual_alternatives == critical_events
    return True, channels_reserved, "accessible-audio"

For repair the audio failure defect, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Audio Failure for bounded accessible audio and cue system.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Audio Failure model · Theory Audio Failure
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Audio Failure: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 7170 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to own mixer initialization, channel budgets, latency, volume and mute controls, and non-audio alternatives for every critical cue; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Continue play with missing files, unavailable mixer, device change, interruption, or mute while preserving diagnostics.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Audio Failure model · Theory Audio Failure.
steps.why: Retrieving Audio Failure through the concrete bounded accessible audio and cue system case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Audio Failure model · Theory Audio Failure, keep Audio Failure connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Continue play with missing files, unavailable mixer, device change, interruption, or mute while preserving diagnostics.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Audio Failure model · Theory Audio Failure, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Audio Failure precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Audio Failure model · Theory Audio Failure, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 7170 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to own mixer initialization, channel budgets, latency, volume and mute controls, and non-audio alternatives for every critical cue; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Continue play with missing files, unavailable mixer, device change, interruption, or mute while preserving diagnostics.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Audio Failure model · Theory Audio Failure, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: A game without audio output should refuse to start.
